content.assessment: This proposal directly targets network capacity and footprint by capping or reducing state-sector 6/8-year gimnázium places. From a network-planning perspective, this is the most directly network-relevant proposal among the five, since it explicitly manipulates capacity variables. However, the geographic concentration of these tracks means uniform national score thresholds and caps will have geographically uneven effects, and the existence of a non-state sector outside the jurisdiction of the contraction policy creates a network-resilience gap that could undermine the intended capacity reduction.
opportunities: Demographic decline provides a structural opportunity to justify capacity contraction without requiring dramatic administrative disruption, easing network transition
strengths: Directly engages the capacity lever identified in the evidence base: current state-sector capacity has remained stagnant despite falling cohorts, so caps could produce a genuine network-level contraction
Uses ministerial discretionary approval power already exercised to deny track expansion requests, an existing administrative capacity within the network governance structure
threats: Church and foundation schools operate outside Klebelsberg jurisdiction and could absorb displaced demand, expanding their own selective tracks and undermining net network-level contraction, since state-sector caps do not constrain this alternative capacity

Contraction concentrated in state-sector capacity without addressing the network's existing urban-concentration bias could further advantage already-dominant flagship schools if remaining places are not geographically redistributed
weaknesses: Uniform national score thresholds interact unevenly with the pre-existing geographic concentration of selective tracks in urban/Budapest areas, likely intensifying rather than equalizing access disparities across the network
Binding thresholds risk reducing places specifically in ways that push students into vocational tracks, as parents' groups previously warned regarding the 70-point threshold recommendation
